sourceMovie.title: My Suicide
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: My Suicide (2009) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. David Lee Miller's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Saltburn
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If My Suicide worked for you, Saltburn (2023) is a strong next pick. The two films share comedy and drama throughlines and comic timing and sharper banter, even though the stories go in different directions.

recommendations.title: Jump In!
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: My Suicide and Jump In! (2007) are not the same movie, but they rhyme in the ways that matter. Each commits to comedy and drama, brings comic timing and sharper banter, and trusts the audience to stay with a bold swing.

faq.question: What should I watch after My Suicide?
faq.answer: Try Saltburn, It's Kind of a Funny Story, Wild Child—each shares DNA with My Suicide while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is My Suicide?
faq.answer: My Suicide (2009) is best described as Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is My Suicide worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
